title: 👤 Per-User Copilot Metrics [Edu] [v2]
description: Shows per-user Copilot activity metrics — interactions, code volume, acceptance rate, feature adoption, and daily trends — filtered to a selected user via the variable picker.

list.name: user_login
list.label: User
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT user_login FROM copilot_user_metrics ORDER BY user_login
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT user_login FROM copilot_user_metrics ORDER BY user_login

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 👤 Per-User Copilot Metrics

**Supplementary Metrics — Individual Developer Copilot Activity**

Use the **User** dropdown above to select a developer and explore their Copilot activity across the selected time range.

## What it Measures
This dashboard surfaces per-user data from the GitHub Copilot Metrics Reports API (users-28-day report). It shows how actively a specific developer is using Copilot — broken down by interaction volume, lines of code suggested and accepted, acceptance rate, and which Copilot surfaces they engage with.

## Why it Matters
Org-level dashboards show aggregate trends, but individual coaching conversations require user-level context. This dashboard helps engineering managers and developer coaches:
- Identify developers who have a seat but are not actively using Copilot
- Distinguish low-acceptance-rate users (may need prompting guidance) from high-volume users
- Understand feature adoption — does the developer use only completions, or also chat, CLI, agent mode, and code review?

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **Total Interactions** | Growing week-over-week | Flat or declining — explore blockers |
| **Acceptance Rate** | ≥30% | <20% — review prompting habits or suggestion quality |
| **Active Days** | Most days in window | Gaps — check for availability or disengagement |
| **Feature Adoption** | Multiple surfaces (✅ on several)  | Only completions — expand use-case awareness |

panels.description: Per-User Copilot Metrics — individual activity, code volume, acceptance rate, and feature adoption.

panels.title: Total Interactions (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(user_initiated_interaction_count) AS "Total Interactions (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `user_initiated_interaction_count` | Tables: `copilot_user_metrics`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10): `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `user_initiated_interaction_count`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of `user_initiated_interaction_count` for the selected user across the Grafana time range. Each row represents one calendar day. An interaction is any user-driven Copilot event: accepting a suggestion, sending a chat message, running a CLI command, etc.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **28-day rolling report** — the `users-28-day` report always covers the most recent 28 days relative to when the sync ran. If you set the Grafana time picker to a different window, rows outside the report window may not exist in the DB.
- **CLI now included** — as of April 10, 2026, CLI activity is included in top-level interaction counts. Historical rows (pre-April 10) reflect IDE-only counts, so be careful comparing trends across that date.
- **Nullable field** — if the API returns null for a day, the row is still inserted with a null value. SUM(null) + number = number in PostgreSQL, so nulls are treated as zero.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- The quality or outcome of interactions.
- Whether interactions led to code being committed.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(user_initiated_interaction_count) AS "Total Interactions (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(code_generation_activity_count) AS "Code Generation Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `code_generation_activity_count`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of `code_generation_activity_count` for the selected user. This counts Copilot code generation events — completions shown to the user, regardless of whether they were accepted.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Shown ≠ Accepted** — this counts how many times Copilot offered a code suggestion, not how many were kept.
- **CLI included from Apr 10, 2026** — CLI-triggered code generation now adds to this count. Cross-date comparisons may show a step change.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How many of these suggestions were accepted.
- Which IDE or language generated the most suggestions.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(code_generation_activity_count) AS "Code Generation Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(code_acceptance_activity_count) AS "Code Acceptance Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `code_acceptance_activity_count`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of `code_acceptance_activity_count` for the selected user. This counts the number of times the user explicitly accepted a Copilot code suggestion (Tab / ✓).

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Accept event, not code survival** — a developer can accept and immediately delete the suggestion. This is still counted as an acceptance.
- **Partial acceptance not tracked** — accepting the first line of a multi-line suggestion and deleting the rest counts as one acceptance.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How many lines of code those acceptances represent (see Lines Accepted below).
- Whether the accepted code survived to production.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(code_acceptance_activity_count) AS "Code Acceptance Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT day) AS "Active Days (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Count of distinct calendar days where the user has a row in `copilot_user_metrics`. The sync stores one row per user per day only when the user had Copilot activity. Days with no activity have no row.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Inactive days are absent, not zero** — a user with 5 active days and a user with 5 zero-activity rows cannot be distinguished without checking for missing rows.
- **Depends on sync cadence** — if syncs are infrequent, recent days may not yet have data.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How productive those active days were.
- Whether inactivity was due to PTO, oncall, or disengagement.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT day) AS "Active Days (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of lines Copilot proposed to add for this user across all days in the time range. Includes both accepted and rejected suggestions.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Suggested ≠ kept** — this includes suggestions the developer dismissed.
- **1 suggestion can span many lines** — a single Tab keystroke accepting a function body could add 20+ lines.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How many of those lines were accepted.
- Which suggestion prompted the most lines (see Acceptance Rate).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Sum of lines the user accepted from Copilot suggestions. This is the numerator of the acceptance rate. A line is counted at the moment of acceptance, not when the code is committed.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Accept ≠ commit** — accepted code may be deleted before commit.
- **CLI merged in** — as of April 10, 2026, CLI-generated lines are included. Pre-April comparisons should note the step change.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Code quality or test coverage of accepted lines.
- Whether accepted lines reached production.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0), 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

`SUM(loc_added_sum) / SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum)` expressed as a percentage. This is a line-weighted acceptance rate — a suggestion of 20 lines counts 20× more than a single-line suggestion.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Lines, not suggestions** — two developers with the same 30% rate may behave differently: one accepts many small suggestions, the other rarely accepts but those suggestions are large.
- **Zero-division handled** — uses `NULLIF(..., 0)` to return NULL instead of dividing by zero when there are no suggestions.
- **Healthy range** — industry benchmarks suggest 25–40% is typical. Very high rates (>70%) may mean the developer only triggers Copilot when they plan to accept; very low rates (<10%) may indicate suggestions are off-target.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether accepted lines are correct or high-quality.
- Why the developer rejected suggestions (irrelevant, incorrect, or just preferred typing).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0), 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Feature Adoption (used at least once in window)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_agent) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Agent Mode (IDE)",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_chat) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Chat",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_cli) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "CLI",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_copilot_code_review_active) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Code Review (Active)",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_copilot_code_review_passive) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Code Review (Passive)",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_copilot_coding_agent) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Cloud Agent"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `used_agent`, `used_chat`, `used_cli`, `used_copilot_code_review_active`, `used_copilot_code_review_passive`, `used_copilot_coding_agent`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Each boolean column is aggregated with `BOOL_OR()` across all rows in the time range. If the user had at least one day with the flag set to `true`, the column shows ✅.

### Feature Definitions

| Feature | Column | Set `true` when... |
|---|---|---|
| **Agent Mode (IDE)** | `used_agent` | Developer used Copilot agent mode inside an IDE |
| **Chat** | `used_chat` | Developer used Copilot Chat (IDE sidebar or web) |
| **CLI** | `used_cli` | Developer used GitHub Copilot CLI (`gh copilot`) |
| **Code Review (Active)** | `used_copilot_code_review_active` | Developer intentionally assigned Copilot as reviewer, re-requested a CCR review, or applied a CCR suggestion |
| **Code Review (Passive)** | `used_copilot_code_review_passive` | Copilot auto-reviewed a PR via repo policy; developer did not interact. Active takes precedence if both occur. |
| **Cloud Agent** | `used_copilot_coding_agent` | Developer assigned Copilot to a GitHub Issue or mentioned @copilot in a PR |

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Day-level granularity** — each boolean reflects whether the feature was used *at all* on a given day. Frequency within a day is not captured.
- **Active vs Passive CCR** — if a user has both active and passive CCR on the same day, only `used_copilot_code_review_active` is set. `BOOL_OR(used_copilot_code_review_active)` being ✅ is a stronger signal of engagement than passive only.
- **Cloud Agent field** — added to the API in the March 25, 2026 changelog. Historic rows before that date will have NULL for this column.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- How frequently each surface was used (not counts, only flags).
- Which IDE the developer used for agent or chat sessions.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_agent) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Agent Mode (IDE)",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_chat) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Chat",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_cli) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "CLI",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_copilot_code_review_active) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Code Review (Active)",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_copilot_code_review_passive) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Code Review (Passive)",
  CASE WHEN BOOL_OR(used_copilot_coding_agent) THEN '✅' ELSE '❌' END AS "Cloud Agent"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE(loc_added_sum, 0) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) AS "Lines Suggested"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`, `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Two time series plotted per calendar day:
- **Lines Accepted** (solid green) — `loc_added_sum` — lines accepted by the user that day
- **Lines Suggested** (dashed blue) — `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` — lines Copilot offered

The gap between the two lines is the volume of rejected suggestions. Shrinking gap = improving acceptance rate.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Gaps = no activity days** — the `users-28-day` report only includes days where the user had activity. Days with no Copilot usage have no row, which creates gaps in the time series.
- **Spike/drop patterns** — sharp spikes on single days often reflect large multi-file refactors where Copilot made bulk suggestions.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Which projects or files were being worked on.
- Whether the accepted lines were later reverted.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT day AS time, COALESCE(loc_added_sum, 0) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.title: Median PR Review Time (hrs)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1
) AS "Median PR Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

panels.title: P90 PR Review Time (hrs)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1
) AS "P90 PR Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

panels.title: PRs Merged

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "PRs Merged"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

GitHub REST API — Pull Requests endpoint.

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

**PR Review Time** = `merged_at − created_at` — elapsed time from when a PR was opened (code already committed on branch) to when it was merged.

Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` — **PRs this developer personally merged**, regardless of who wrote the code. Captures both their own PRs and Copilot-agent PRs they reviewed and shipped.

- **Median (P50)** — half of PRs merged faster, half slower. Resistant to outliers.
- **P90** — 90% of PRs merged within this time. Highlights the slow tail.
- **Count** — total PRs merged in the selected window.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Author-agnostic by design** — includes human-authored and Copilot-bot-authored PRs. Use the Recent PRs table to see the `Author` column and distinguish them.
- **`created_at` is reliable** — GitHub requires at least one commit to open a PR, so `created_at` always means code was ready for review, not just that a branch existed.
- **Long-running branches skew P90** — a feature branch open for weeks will inflate the P90 significantly.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Whether the review was thorough or rubber-stamped.
- How many review cycles happened before merge.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1
) AS "Median PR Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN \$__timeFrom() AND \$__timeTo()
```

panels.title: Weekly PR Review Time Trend

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  ROUND(
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
      ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
    )::numeric, 1
  ) AS "Median Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

panels.title: Weekly PR Review Time Trend — Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

GitHub REST API — Pull Requests endpoint.

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Weekly median of `merged_at − created_at` for PRs merged by this user. Bucketed by `date_trunc('week', merged_at)`.

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Low-volume weeks** — a week with 1 PR will show exactly that PR's review time with no averaging.
- **Week boundary artifacts** — a PR opened in one week and merged the next appears only in the merge week.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- Why review time changed week-over-week (team bandwidth, PR complexity, Copilot vs human authorship).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  ROUND(
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
      ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
    )::numeric, 1
  ) AS "Median Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN \$__timeFrom() AND \$__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
```

panels.title: Recent PRs Merged by This User

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  pr.number AS "PR #",
  pr.title AS "Title",
  pr.user_login AS "Author",
  ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0, 1) AS "Review Time (hrs)",
  pr.created_at AS "Opened",
  pr.merged_at AS "Merged"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY pr.merged_at DESC
LIMIT 25

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source

GitHub REST API — Pull Requests endpoint.

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

25 most recently merged PRs where `merged_by_login = user`. The **Author** column distinguishes human-written PRs from Copilot-agent PRs (bot login). Review Time = `merged_at − created_at` in hours, colour-coded green (<24h) / yellow (24–72h) / red (>72h).

### ⚠️ Calculation Challenges

- **Author = PR opener** — for Copilot coding agent PRs the author will be the bot username, not the human developer.
- **Sorted by merge date** — most recent first. Click the Review Time column header to sort by slowest.

### 💡 What This Metric Doesn't Tell You

- The quality of the review.
- How many back-and-forth cycles occurred.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT pr.number AS "PR #", pr.title AS "Title", pr.user_login AS "Author",
  ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0, 1) AS "Review Time (hrs)",
  pr.created_at AS "Opened", pr.merged_at AS "Merged"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN \$__timeFrom() AND \$__timeTo()
ORDER BY pr.merged_at DESC LIMIT 25
```
